welcomeMessage.en: You did a great job yesterday!
Today, we are going to talk about the things you love doing in your free time.
welcomeMessage.zh: 今天，我们将谈论你在空闲时间喜欢做的事情。

welcomeMessage.en: Hobbies are a great way to relax, and it is always fun to talk about the things we enjoy.
welcomeMessage.zh: 爱好是放松的好方法，谈论我们喜欢的事情总是很有趣的。

questionsToThinkAbout.en: What is your favorite way to relax after a long, busy day?
questionsToThinkAbout.zh: 在漫长而忙碌的一天之后，你最喜欢的放松方式是什么？

questionsToThinkAbout.en: Do you prefer indoor activities (like reading) or outdoor activities (like hiking)?
questionsToThinkAbout.zh: 你更喜欢室内活动（比如阅读）还是户外活动（比如远足）？

questionsToThinkAbout.en: Is there a new hobby or skill you want to try in the future?
questionsToThinkAbout.zh: 未来有没有你想尝试的新爱好或技能？

vocabulary.definition: An activity that you do for pleasure when you are not working.
vocabulary.example: My favorite hobby is playing the guitar.

vocabulary.definition: Time when you do not have to work or study.
vocabulary.example: In my free time, I like to read.

vocabulary.definition: Something that you enjoy learning about or doing.
vocabulary.example: I have a strong interest in history.
Listening to music helps me relax.

vocabulary.definition: Happening inside a building / happening outside in nature.
vocabulary.example: I prefer outdoor sports like soccer.

vocabulary.definition: A natural ability to do something well.
vocabulary.example: She has a talent for painting.

vocabulary.definition: To do something again and again to get better at it.
To buy or find things of a certain type and keep them.
vocabulary.example: When I was young, I used to collect stamps.
I tried to learn the piano, but I gave up.

goals: Encourage details: When people talk about things they love, they usually have a lot to say! If they give a short answer, ask 'Why?' or 'How exactly do you do that?'
Praise their effort: Remind them that they are doing a great job speaking for a second day in a row.

Grammar focus (mental note): Pay attention to the Present Simple tense since hobbies are usually habits (e.g., 'I play tennis', 'She reads books'). Watch out for the missing 's' on third-person verbs if they talk about someone else's hobbies!

phases.title: Phase 1: Warm-Up Questions (10-15 Minutes)
phases.description: Start with easy, factual questions to break the ice.
questions: How was your day today? Did you have any free time?
What do you usually do on a typical Sunday afternoon?
What are your main hobbies right now?
Did you have different hobbies when you were a child compared to now?
Do you prefer doing your hobbies alone, or with other people?

phases.title: Phase 2: Deep Dive Questions (30-40 Minutes)
phases.description: These require longer, more thoughtful answers. Ask follow-up questions based on what they say.
questions: The Origin: Think about your absolute favorite hobby. How did you first get interested in it? Who introduced you to it?
The Feeling: How does doing your hobby make you feel? (For example, does it make you feel energetic, peaceful, or focused?)
Dream Hobbies: If you had unlimited money and unlimited free time, what is one brand new hobby you would want to start? (e.g., Flying planes, deep-sea diving?)
Boring to You: Are there any popular hobbies that a lot of people love, but you think are completely boring? Why?
Hidden Talents: Do you have any 'hidden talents' or useless skills that most people don't know about?
Time Management: In modern life, many people say they are 'too busy' for hobbies. Do you think it is important to make time for hobbies? Why?

phases.title: Phase 3: Fun Roleplay (5-10 Minutes)
phases.description: If you have extra time or want to make them laugh, try this scenario.
phases.scenario: Tell your friend: 'Imagine I am someone who is very stressed from work, and I have zero hobbies. I just sit on the couch and stare at the wall. Your job is to persuade me to try YOUR favorite hobby. Tell me why it is so great, what I need to buy to get started, and convince me to join you this weekend!'
